reasoning: A typical 1-week stock movement has a base rate probability of around 51.5% to go up. However, Dollar General (DG) reported earnings on March 12, 2026, and its FY26 guidance caused the stock to gap down significantly, falling from 144.15 (Mar 11) to 131.21 (Mar 13). The well-documented post-earnings announcement drift (PEAD) suggests that stocks often experience persisted negative momentum in the days and weeks following a severe downward shock. Furthermore, we confirmed that DG's next ex-dividend date is April 7, 2026, meaning there will strictly be no mechanical price reduction this coming week. Thus, the prediction is essentially a one-week momentum bet. Given the recent steep sell-off, PEAD effects, and yfinance's combined statistical estimate (44%), we lower the probability below a coin toss, but maintain it near 47% because short-term stock movements are still largely unpredictable random walks.
